codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, commit diff, and the resulting `src/clients/statcast_client.py` / `tests/test_statcast_client.py`. The implementation covers daily Statcast parquet storage, batting/pitching parquet persistence, and OAA merging, but two defensive ingestion entry points still persist qualified-only leaderboards instead of raw season data, and the generic team game log wrapper is narrowed to batting-only output.

issues.description: `fetch_fielding_stats()` calls `fielding_stats(season)` with no qualification override. In pybaseball, Fangraphs fetches default `qual` to `'y'` when it is omitted (`.venv/Lib/site-packages/pybaseball/datasources/fangraphs.py:138`), so the saved parquet contains only qualified fielders instead of the raw defensive dataset requested by the feature. That will drop many part-time and early-season defenders needed for downstream DRS/OAA features.

`fetch_catcher_framing()` delegates straight to `statcast_catcher_framing(season)`, and pybaseball's API defaults `min_called_p` to `'q'` (`.venv/Lib/site-packages/pybaseball/statcast_fielding.py:153-154`). That means the persisted parquet omits unqualified catchers rather than storing raw framing data, which breaks the feature's requirement to store raw data and will leave many projected catchers missing, especially early in the season.

`fetch_team_game_logs()` hard-codes `log_type="batting"` even though pybaseball's underlying `team_game_logs()` supports both batting and pitching logs. Because the wrapper is named generically and the parquet path does not encode log type, consumers cannot ingest pitching logs for bullpen-usage workflows without changing the API/storage contract later.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared lint/typecheck commands and the `data-worker` skill still prescribe the shell-glob form `python -m py_compile src/**/*.py`, even though this repo's Windows/PowerShell workflow requires a different syntax in practice.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/services.yaml:6,9` define both `typecheck` and `lint` as `python -m py_compile src/**/*.py`; `.factory/skills/data-worker/SKILL.md:45` repeats the same command; the handoff `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-19T15-19-58-851Z__t6-pybaseball-ingestion__8d3911e1-91c8-4c03-8476-773665162558.json` records a justified deviation to `python -m compileall src tests` because the glob form was unreliable in PowerShell.

summary: Review failed. The Statcast/batting/pitching ingestion pieces are in place, but `fetch_fielding_stats()` and `fetch_catcher_framing()` both persist qualified-only leaderboards rather than raw defensive data, which does not fully satisfy the feature contract. I also noted one shared-state gap: the shared lint/typecheck commands still use a PowerShell-fragile `py_compile` glob pattern.
